title: High-memory partition memory bandwidth topology
difficulty_justification: Requires reading hardware specs and NUMA topology details, combining memory bandwidth figures with partition configuration.
query_text: What is the memory bandwidth and NUMA topology for nodes in the high-memory partition? Would these nodes be suitable for in-memory analytics workloads?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: High-memory partition (highmem) nodes have: 128 cores (Intel Xeon Platinum 8380), 2048 GB RAM, 4 NUMA nodes per server, and 204.8 GB/s aggregate memory bandwidth (~51.2 GB/s per NUMA domain). These nodes are well-suited for in-memory analytics (e.g. Spark/Dask with large datasets) due to the 2 TB capacity per node. NUMA-aware placement matters: for a single threaded application, bandwidth per NUMA node is 51.2 GB/s; for full-node jobs, bind with --cpu-bind=cores and use numactl for data placement to avoid cross-NUMA traffic. Current utilization is 63% so slots are generally available.

checkpoints.description: Agent describes 4 NUMA nodes per server.
